steps.title: Identify the two verb problem
steps.content:
The sentence has two potential verbs: compared and found. A grammatical sentence needs exactly one main verb per clause. Since found Milvus migrans eggs to be longer is clearly the core finding being reported, found must be the main verb — which means compared the lengths of eggs produced by different bird species must be a relative clause modifying study. That requires a relative pronoun directly after study.
  • Choice A — study, that: Adds a comma before that. Restrictive relative clauses (introduced by that) never take a comma. This is grammatically incorrect. ✗
  • Choice B — study, which: Adds a comma, making it a non-restrictive clause. But which with a comma would make found a second main verb dangling without proper connection. ✗
  • Choice C — study: No relative pronoun at all. This leaves both compared and found as competing main verbs with no grammatical link between them. ✗
  • Choice D — study that: A restrictive relative clause with no comma. That compared the lengths... modifies study, and found becomes the main verb of the sentence. Grammatically correct. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: "Study that" correctly introduces a restrictive relative clause, letting found serve as the main verb without punctuation errors. D is correct.
